# UC-22.14.40 · Information Input Validation — Web Parameter Anomalies

## Description

Surfaces WAF or app logs showing validation failures and injection patterns for SI-10 information input validation.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes web and sourcetypes apache:access`, f5:asm:*, f5:asm:*`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize field names per TA; (2) tune out scanner noise with bot lookup; (3) feed IR-4 for confirmed exploitation attempts; (4) map to RA-5 findings. Indexes required: `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=apache:access``, `sourcetype=f5:asm:*`, `sourcetype=f5:asm:*``, `sourcetype=modsec_json`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP ASM (TA), ModSecurity, or `access_combined` with WAF headers.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=f5:asm:*` or `sourcetype=apache:access` with `mod_security` message route.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field names per TA; (2) tune out scanner noise with bot lookup; (3) feed IR-4 for confirmed exploitation attempts; (4) map to RA-5 findings.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype=f5:asm:* OR sourcetype=modsec_json earliest=-24h
| search severity IN ("high","critical") OR response_code=406
| stats count by uri, src_ip, attack_type
| sort - count
| head 100
```

Understanding this SPL

**Information Input Validation — Web Parameter Anomalies (SI-10)** — Surfaces WAF or app logs showing validation failures and injection patterns for SI-10 information input validation.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=f5:asm:*` or `sourcetype=apache:access` with `mod_security` message route. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP ASM (TA), ModSecurity, or `access_combined` with WAF headers. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: f5:asm:*, modsec_json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype=f5:asm:*, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by uri, src_ip, attack_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (top URIs), Map (src_ip), Time chart (attack types)

## SPL

```spl
index=web sourcetype=f5:asm:* OR sourcetype=modsec_json earliest=-24h
| search severity IN ("high","critical") OR response_code=406
| stats count by uri, src_ip, attack_type
| sort - count
| head 100
```

## Visualization

Table (top URIs), Map (src_ip), Time chart (attack types)
